design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed(super-vs-siri|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Siri|2026-07-06T10:45:07.637655+00:00) -> hero=editorial-split; AIDA Check: Attention(hero contrast), Interest(comparison+bento), Desire(motion+media), Action(CTA band); Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk; Hero Math Verification: clamp(44px–72px) max-width 1120px = 2–3 lines; Bento Density Verification: grid-auto-flow:dense confirmed; GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics; Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA / white secondary CTA verified
headline: Super vs Siri — personal AI agents compared for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Siri. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Siri

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison of Super vs Siri for people deciding how far they want AI to go beyond voice commands. Siri is improving as a device‑embedded assistant, while Super is built for durable computer‑use workflows with a reusable computer-use cache that makes repeated work cheaper and more reliable.
